description: Cyber defence, ethical hacking, digital forensics and information-security careers.

careers.name: Cybersecurity Analyst
careers.overview: Cybersecurity Analyst protects digital systems, networks, data and users from cyber threats through security testing and defence.
careers.whyChoose: Cybersecurity Analyst is useful because it is a fast-growing, high-responsibility field with demand across technology, banking, government and consulting.
careers.eligibility: Class 12 with Mathematics or Computer Science is useful; B.Tech/BCA/B.Sc IT and certifications are common.
careers.educationPath: Class 12 -> CS/IT/cybersecurity degree or certification -> Labs/projects -> Security role.
careers.workEnvironment: Security operations centers, IT companies, banks, consulting firms, government cyber units and product companies.
careers.futureScope: Excellent due to digital payments, cloud, data privacy, cybercrime and organizational security needs.
careers.aiImpact: AI will support threat detection while attackers also use AI, increasing demand for skilled security professionals.
cons: Early career may need portfolio or training
